Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001

Automorphisms of Aschbacher Graphs

Algebra and Logic 40 (2) (2001), 69–74. DOI: 10.1023/A:1010217919915.

6 formalized · 9 pending · 0 out of scope · total 15 · all papers · browse source on GitHub

Lemma 1 — strong fix

1 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 2

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001, Lemma 1 — 1/2 formalized, 1 pending .lean
  • Lemma 1 (1) (strong (0,1) ⇒ regular or star). — Any finite strong-`(λ = 0, µ = 1)` graph is either regular (every vertex has the same degree, the "Moore graph" branch) or a star with some centre. The Moore-graph branch of the paper's formulation is captured here by… Pending deferred L44
  • Lemma 1 (2) (`Fix(σ)` is also strong (0,1), single-element case). — For a Moore57 graph Γ and any σ ∈ Aut(Γ), the σ-fixed induced subgraph satisfies the strong (λ = 0, µ = 1) condition Formalized auto L59

Lemma 2 — involution fix

1 formalized · 0 pending · 0 out of scope · total 1

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001, Lemma 2 — 1/1 formalized .lean
  • Lemma 2 (involution fixes a star). — For a Moore57 graph Γ and a non-trivial involution `σ ∈ Aut(Γ)`, the σ-fixed induced subgraph is a star with some center `c`. Wraps `aut_involution_fixedInducedGraph_isStarWithCenter` Formalized auto L37

Lemma 3 — odd-prime fix

2 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 3

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001, Lemma 3 — 2/3 formalized, 1 pending .lean
  • Lemma 3 (general 6-way classification). Pending deferred L43
  • Lemma 3, case (2): order 19, `|Fix| = 1`. — Any order-19 automorphism of Moore57 has exactly one fixed vertex Formalized auto L47
  • Lemma 3, case (4): order 11, `|Fix|` is a pentagon (5 vertices). — Any order-11 automorphism of Moore57 has fixed-vertex set of size 5 (induced subgraph is the pentagon `C₅`) Formalized auto L56

Lemma 4 — involution good

1 formalized · 0 pending · 0 out of scope · total 1

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001, Lemma 4 — 1/1 formalized .lean
  • Lemma 4 (involution is "good": `|Fix(t)| = 56`). — Every non-trivial involution of a Moore57 graph fixes exactly 56 vertices — this is the `k = 57` instance of the paper's "good" definition `|Fix(t)| = k − 1` Formalized auto L38

Lemmas 5–9 — involution structure

0 formalized · 5 pending · 0 out of scope · total 5

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001, Lemmas 5–9 (involution-centric structure) [deferred-heavy] — 0/5 formalized, 5 pending .lean
  • Lemma 5 (transposed `b, c ∈ [a]` adjacency). Pending deferred L32
  • Lemma 6 (no second involution in the same neighbourhood). Pending deferred L35
  • Lemma 7 (centraliser of `t` odd elements). Pending deferred L38
  • Lemma 8 (second involution `s ≠ t` constraints). Pending deferred L41
  • Lemma 9 (final structure constraints). Pending deferred L44

Main theorem

1 formalized · 2 pending · 0 out of scope · total 3

Makhnev–Paduchikh 2001 — main theorem — 1/3 formalized, 2 pending .lean
  • Main theorem (1) (`Fix(t)` is a 56-vertex star). — For any non-trivial involution `t ∈ Aut(Γ)`, `|Fix(t)| = 56` and `Fix(t)` is a star (centered at some vertex) Formalized auto L55
  • Main theorem (2) (decomposition `G = ⟨Y, t⟩ × X`). Pending deferred L66
  • Main theorem (3) (`Fix(X)` cases when `X ≠ 1`). Pending deferred L69
Source & Lean proofs: github.com/yawara/moore57. Maintained by Yawara Ishida.
